stories.headline: A DAY'S EVENTS IN LONDON; LORD SALISBURY TO VISIT THE QUEEN TO-MORROW. CHARGES OF CORRUPTION IN THE ORDNANCE DEPARTMENT IGNORED BY THE WAR SECRETARY. MRS. CRAWFORD'S SHAME. SIR CHARLES DILKE EXCITED OVER THE SUMMING UP. JUDGE J.F. DALY'S AFFLICTION. CURRENT FOREIGN TOPICS.
stories.summary: Lord Salisbury is scheduled to meet with the Queen and his cabinet colleagues after arriving in London. He will then travel to Osborne on Saturday.

stories.headline: KNIGHTS OF LABOR CAUGHT; CONFESSING THEY WRECKED A FREIGHT TRAIN. FIVE OF THEM ARRESTED ON A CHARGE OF MURDERING A MISSOURI PACIFIC FIREMAN AND BRAKEMAN.
stories.summary: Five members of the Knights of Labor were arrested and confessed to wrecking a freight train and murdering a fireman and brakeman during a strike.

stories.headline: UNREST AGAIN IN FRANCE; THOUGHTFUL MEN ANXIOUS FOR THE REPUBLIC. BLUNDERS IT HAS MADE--ENTHUSIASM OVER THE FETE WANTING--BOULANGER'S POPULARITY, AND GREAT EVENTS THAT MAY BE NEAR AT HAND.
stories.summary: There is unrest in France, with princes being expelled and concerns about the stability of the republic, as well as the popularity of a figure named Boulanger.

stories.headline: THE HATED CASTLEREAGH; LORD SALISBURY'S STUPID BLUNDER AT THE OUTSET. LONDONDERRY'S APPOINTMENT AS LORD LIEUTENANT OF IRELAND--LORD RANDOLPH CHURCHILL'S HAND IN THE NEW POLITICAL GAME.
stories.summary: The Marquis of Londonderry has been appointed as the Lord Lieutenant of Ireland, a move that is seen as part of a new political strategy.

reveal_image.title: Australian cricket team in England in 1886

rounds.blurb: Lord Salisbury met with the Queen and his cabinet colleagues in London. The Knights of Labor were arrested for wrecking a freight train and murdering a fireman and brakeman. Unrest occurred in France, with concerns about the republic's stability.

stories.headline: In New Jersey, Dreams of a Better Life Dashed by Foreclosure Crisis
stories.summary: Many people in New Jersey are losing their homes due to mortgage defaults, especially in areas where subprime loans were common. This is causing a foreclosure crisis in the state.

stories.headline: At Notre Dame, Obama Calls for Civil Tone in Abortion Debate
stories.summary: President Obama gave a speech at Notre Dame about abortion, calling for a more civil discussion, but was met with protests from anti-abortion leaders.

stories.headline: A Dose of Diesel Medicine Without the Nasty Aftertaste
stories.summary: BMW has created a new high-performance diesel vehicle for the American market, but it is expensive.

stories.headline: As Dollars Pile Up, Uneasy Traders Lower the Currency’s Value
stories.summary: The value of the dollar has decreased due to concerns that economic stimulus efforts may lead to inflation, making traders nervous.

rounds.blurb: The foreclosure crisis worsened, causing many New Jersey residents to lose their homes. President Obama called for a civil tone in the abortion debate during a speech at Notre Dame. The dollar's value decreased due to inflation concerns.

stories.headline: Summary; BUSINESS DIGEST; TUESDAY, JANUARY 25, 1983; International
stories.summary: An OPEC meeting in Geneva ended without an agreement on production quotas, which could lead to lower oil prices. Oil company stocks fell sharply as a result.

stories.headline: DEFICIT IN THE $185 BILLION RANGE EXPECTED IN 1984 REAGAN BUDGET
stories.summary: The upcoming budget is expected to have a deficit of around $185 billion, which is higher than the previously set target of $155 billion due to a lengthening recession.

stories.headline: GROWTH FOR INDUSTRIAL ECONOMIES URGED BY U.S. TO EASE DEBT CRISIS
stories.summary: A US policymaker warned that the international debt crisis will worsen unless Western nations and Japan boost their economies to stimulate growth and increase trade.

stories.headline: JUDGE TELLS YANKEES TO PLAY 1983 OPENER IN BRONX, NOT DENVER
stories.summary: A judge ruled that the New York Yankees cannot play their first three home games in Denver, ordering them to play in Yankee Stadium instead.

rounds.blurb: The OPEC meeting in Geneva ended without an agreement on production quotas, potentially leading to lower oil prices. Oil company stocks fell sharply as a result, amidst concerns over the US budget deficit and international debt crisis. The US urged Western nations to boost their economies.
